answer:
EGARCH models address the non-negativity constraint on coefficients (i) by using logged variance, allow for leverage effects (ii) by incorporating asymmetric responses to shocks, and handle symmetric responsiveness (iii) by design. However, EGARCH does not inherently allow for a feedback from volatility to returns (iv), which is typically addressed by GARCH-in-mean models. Thus, criticisms (i), (ii), and (iii) are overcome by EGARCH.

\boxed{E}
